babyDevelopment: The fetus is officially moving! Although you can't feel it, the baby is stretching and kicking. External genitalia are forming, though it's usually too early to see on ultrasound. The diaphragm is developing, preparing the baby for future breathing.
bodyChanges: Your metabolism is increasing, which may make you feel warmer. Hormonal changes might improve your skin and hair ('pregnancy glow'). Some women experience 'linea nigra'—a dark line appearing on the abdomen.
tips: Stay hydrated as your blood volume continues to rise
Moisturize your belly to manage skin tightness
Keep up with regular, moderate exercise

do: Eat calcium-rich foods for baby's bone development
Get enough Vitamin C to support tissue repair
Rest when your body signals fatigue
dont: Overheat in hot showers or baths
Skip meals, even if feeling better

travelAdvisory.note: A great time for travel before the third trimester.

milestoneDescription: The baby is now stretching and kicking as muscles develop.
